name: Rybbit
description: Privacy-first open-source web analytics with session replays, user journeys, and real-time dashboards. Cookieless, no consent banner needed.

name: Open WebUI
description: Self-hosted ChatGPT-style interface for local and cloud LLMs. Supports Ollama, OpenAI API, RAG, voice input, and multi-model conversations.

name: Tabby
description: Self-hosted GitHub Copilot alternative. Local code completion using open-source models with VS Code and JetBrains support.

name: NocoDB
description: Open-source Airtable alternative that turns any SQL database into a smart spreadsheet with REST API. Self-hostable.

name: AppFlowy
description: Open-source Notion alternative built with Flutter and Rust. Rich text editor, databases, kanban boards, and AI features.

name: Trigger.dev
description: Open-source background jobs platform for TypeScript. Long-running tasks with retries, queues, and no timeouts. Self-hostable.

name: Windmill
description: Open-source workflow engine combining visual builder with full code support. Scripts, flows, and apps in Python, TypeScript, Go, SQL.

name: Activepieces
description: Open-source no-code business automation platform. Zapier alternative with 280+ integrations and MCP server support. MIT licensed.

name: DevPod
description: Open-source Codespaces alternative. Reproducible dev environments on any infrastructure — Kubernetes, AWS, GCP, or local Docker.

name: ATAC
description: Feature-full TUI API client built in Rust. Free, offline, no account required. Postman alternative for the terminal.

name: sqlit
description: Lazygit-style TUI for SQL databases. Supports 25+ databases from Postgres to Snowflake with vim keybindings and fuzzy search.

name: sql-tap
description: Sits between app and database, capturing every SQL query in a TUI. Auto-detects N+1 query patterns with zero config.

name: code2prompt
description: Converts entire codebases into structured LLM prompts. Token counting, smart filtering, template system, and git integration.

name: Pangolin
description: Identity-aware tunneled reverse proxy with built-in auth and web dashboard. Exposes self-hosted services via VPS without port forwarding.

name: Postiz
description: Self-hosted social media scheduling tool. 20+ platforms, calendar views, AI post generation, RSS auto-posting, and team collaboration.

name: termscp
description: Terminal file transfer TUI supporting SCP, SFTP, FTP, S3, SMB, WebDAV, and Kubernetes. Swiss-army knife for remote file access.

name: Colanode
description: Local-first open-source workspace combining real-time chat, notes, calendar, and tasks. Notion + Slack in one self-hosted app.

name: Hoodik
description: End-to-end encrypted self-hosted cloud storage built with Rust. 2048-bit RSA + AES encryption using under 20MB RAM.

name: Forge
description: 3MB Rust binary that coordinates multi-AI coding agents via MCP. Provider-agnostic, supports 300+ models as an AI pair programmer.

name: Uncloud
description: Lightweight container orchestration without a control plane. Peer-to-peer state sync via distributed SQLite over WireGuard mesh.

name: gonzo
description: Real-time log analysis TUI with charts, AI-powered anomaly detection, severity visualization, and word clouds.

name: serie
description: Rich git commit graph rendered in terminal using image protocols (iTerm2, Kitty). Beautiful branch visualization without leaving the CLI.

name: BookLore
description: Self-hosted digital library with built-in EPUB/PDF reader, Kobo sync, smart shelves, and multi-user support.

name: Ziit
description: Open-source self-hostable WakaTime alternative for coding time tracking. Clean dashboard, GitHub login, embeddable badges.

name: Appwrite Sites
description: Open-source Vercel alternative for deploying static and SSR web apps. Git integration, preview deployments, and global CDN.

name: Tinyauth
description: 20MB authentication middleware for reverse proxies. OAuth support for GitHub and Google. Works with Traefik, Nginx, and Caddy.

name: Forward Email
description: 100% open-source privacy-focused email service. All-in-one SMTP, IMAP, and email forwarding. Self-hostable Gmail alternative.

name: Arcane
description: Modern Docker management UI with Compose editing, GitOps, web terminal, and mobile-responsive dashboard. Portainer alternative.

name: moulti
description: CLI-driven TUI that organizes command outputs into collapsible, searchable blocks. Perfect for long shell scripts and Ansible runs.

name: Portname
description: Auto-detects your framework and gives every dev server a stable named localhost URL instead of numeric ports.

name: Hive Memory
description: MCP server providing persistent cross-project memory for AI coding agents. Stores decisions, learnings, and context locally.

name: Warracker
description: Self-hosted warranty tracker. Store receipts, monitor expiration dates, get alerts. Integrates with Paperless-ngx.

name: Papra
description: Minimalist self-hosted document archiver with email ingestion, auto-tagging, OCR, and folder ingestion. Lighter than Paperless-ngx.

name: ByteStash
description: Self-hosted code snippet manager with creation, editing, and filtering. SQLite-backed, Docker deploy. Private Gist alternative.

name: Palmr
description: Privacy-focused self-hosted file sharing like WeTransfer. Temporary links, expiration, passwords, S3-compatible storage.

name: Lurker
description: Self-hosted read-only Reddit frontend. Browse and save subreddits without an account or tracking. SQLite-backed.

name: Upvote RSS
description: Generate rich RSS feeds from Reddit, Hacker News, Lemmy, and Lobsters. Optional AI summaries via any LLM provider.

name: mcp2cli
description: Turn any MCP server or OpenAPI spec into a CLI at runtime. Reduces token usage by 96-99% compared to native MCP tool schemas.

name: Hatchet
description: Distributed task queue and DAG-based workflow orchestrator with SDKs for Python, TypeScript, and Go. Self-hostable.

name: OmniTools
description: Self-hosted web app with PDF, image, text, and data utilities. All processing happens client-side in the browser. 28MB Docker image.

name: Dayflow
description: macOS app that watches your screen with AI and generates a semantic timeline of your workday. Like git log for your day.

name: QitOps
description: Unified CLI for API testing, load testing, security scanning, and data-driven testing in one Rust binary. CI/CD friendly.

name: Claw
description: MCP server that gives AI coding agents bash, read, write, edit, and grep tools on any remote machine via SSH.

name: Seite
description: AI-native static site generator with built-in MCP server. Single Rust binary, generates HTML/RSS/sitemap/llms.txt. No Node required.

name: Databasus
description: Self-hosted database backup tool for PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, and MongoDB. AES-256 encryption, S3/GDrive/Azure targets.

name: xplr
description: Hackable, minimal TUI file explorer with embedded Lua scripting and vim-like modal interface. Infinitely extensible via plugins.

name: stu
description: TUI explorer for Amazon S3 buckets. Browse, preview, and download files from the terminal without the AWS console.

name: Tuckr
description: Super-powered GNU Stow replacement for dotfile management. Cross-platform including Windows. Zero config required.

name: kaskade
description: TUI for Apache Kafka. Browse topics, consume messages with Avro/JSON deserialization, and monitor consumer groups.

name: NoteDiscovery
description: Self-hosted Obsidian-like note-taking. Graph view, wikilinks, LaTeX, Mermaid diagrams, PWA. Stores plain .md files.

name: StarFetch
description: Fast modern system information fetch tool in Rust. Neofetch replacement designed for speed after Neofetch was archived.

name: Raijin
description: Free weather TUI — no API key, no account, no subscription. Uses NWS and OpenMeteo data. Just run it.

name: gitlogue
description: Cinematic git commit replay tool. Animates typing, cursor movement, and syntax highlighting. Great for demos and screencasts.

name: Wanderer
description: Self-hosted trail and hiking route database. Save GPS tracks, plan routes, and organize outdoor adventures. Komoot alternative.

name: Zerobyte
description: Backup tool built on restic with a clean web UI for managing and automating encrypted data backups.

name: Micasa
description: Terminal UI for tracking home projects, maintenance schedules, appliances, and vendor quotes. Single SQLite file, no cloud.

name: Yardstiq
description: CLI that sends one prompt to multiple LLMs simultaneously, streams responses side-by-side, with built-in AI judge scoring.

name: Ansible TUI
description: Zero-dependency terminal UI for interactively selecting hosts and tasks from Ansible playbooks with live command preview.

name: Koreo
description: Platform engineering toolkit for Kubernetes. Meta-controller language that composes operators into cohesive platforms with hot-reloading.

name: thanks-stars
description: CLI that stars all GitHub repos your project depends on. Supports Cargo.toml, package.json, go.mod, and more.
